text: Extending sleep to a minimum goal of 10 hours in bed per night over 5-7 weeks increased total nightly sleep time by approximately 111 minutes compared to habitual sleep schedules.
Sleep extension over 5-7 weeks improved sprint speed in collegiate basketball players, reducing timed sprint from 16.2 to 15.5 seconds (a 4.3% improvement).
Sleep extension improved shooting accuracy, with free throw percentage increasing by 9% and 3-point field goal percentage increasing by 9.2%.
Sleep extension significantly reduced reaction time as measured by the Psychomotor Vigilance Task, indicating improved neurocognitive function.
Sleep extension reduced daytime sleepiness (lower Epworth Sleepiness Scale scores) and improved mood with increased vigor and decreased fatigue.
Athletes who extended their sleep reported improved overall ratings of both physical and mental well-being during practices and games.
